requirements.requirement: Party Mode loads persona identity, role, communication style, principles, and capabilities from approved agent definitions, and user-visible identity is branded as ThinkTank advisor content.

cases: [P0][5.2-UNIT-001][AC1,AC2] loads approved personas and selects differentiated ThinkTank advisors
[P0][5.2-UNIT-001B][AC1] treats name-only team rows as an allowlist backed by manifest source paths
[P0][5.2-UNIT-001C][AC1] rejects invalid advisor count inputs before selecting personas
[P0][5.2-UNIT-002B][AC1,AC3] propagates non-recoverable source approval errors instead of treating them as omissions
[P0][5.2-UNIT-002C][AC1] rejects approved CSV rows that point outside agent definition roots
[P0][5.2-UNIT-003B][AC1] does not fall back to every manifest advisor when team rosters contain no valid approved candidates
[P1][5.2-UNIT-004][AC1,AC2] returns only scalar-safe metadata pointers and source hashes
[P0][5.2-BE-001][AC1,AC2] explains selected ThinkTank advisor personas before Party Mode discussion begins

heuristics.endpoint_coverage: N/A - no new HTTP endpoint or DTO contract introduced
heuristics.auth_authz_coverage: Inherited Story 5.1 feature flag, tenant allowlist, stale decision, and cross-tenant negative paths remain covered in party-mode-entry ATDD
heuristics.error_path_coverage: Covered by invalid count, non-agent path, non-recoverable approval error, manifest/team allowlist, and raw-content leakage tests

requirements.requirement: Party Mode selects relevant and differentiated advisors, then explains selected advisor names and perspectives before discussion begins.

cases: [P0][5.2-UNIT-001][AC1,AC2] loads approved personas and selects differentiated ThinkTank advisors
[P0][5.2-UNIT-001D][AC2] uses step and message relevance when ordering differentiated advisors
[P1][5.2-UNIT-004][AC1,AC2] returns only scalar-safe metadata pointers and source hashes
[P0][5.2-BE-001][AC1,AC2] explains selected ThinkTank advisor personas before Party Mode discussion begins

heuristics.auth_authz_coverage: N/A for advisor selection; Party Mode start availability is covered by Story 5.1 regression tests in the same ATDD suite
heuristics.error_path_coverage: Differentiation and relevance are covered by deterministic unit assertions; no provider call is made

requirements.requirement: If one advisor persona fails to load, Party Mode can continue with the minimum viable set and explains omitted advisors; if fewer than three advisors remain, it fails safely and keeps the start retryable.

cases: [P0][5.2-UNIT-002][AC3] continues with visible omission when one selected advisor source fails and the viable set remains
[P0][5.2-UNIT-002B][AC1,AC3] propagates non-recoverable source approval errors instead of treating them as omissions
[P0][5.2-UNIT-003][AC3] fails closed when fewer than three approved advisors can be loaded
[P0][5.2-UNIT-003C][AC3] explains invalid team roster rows when the viable advisor set remains
[P0][5.2-BE-002][AC3] keeps Party Mode start retryable when advisor loading falls below the minimum viable set
[P1][5.2-BE-003][AC3] surfaces omitted advisor names without exposing source paths or stack traces

heuristics.auth_authz_coverage: N/A for persona loading; existing Party Mode start authorization regressions remain covered
heuristics.error_path_coverage: Covered by recoverable omission, non-recoverable approval failure, below-minimum failure, retryable rollback, and message cleanup assertions

recommendations.action: Keep the Story 5.2 runtime and Party Mode entry suites in the Epic 5 regression set because they guard file-source approval, metadata sanitization, and rollback behavior.

verification_commands: cd backend && npm run test -- src/modules/advisory/runtime --runInBand
